attractions.name: Block 16
attractions.description: Block 16 is a locally-owned, farm-to-table street food restaurant in downtown Omaha known for its bold, imaginative, and "deliciously messy" dishes. The kitchen emphasizes high-quality, locally sourced, and preservative-free ingredients, many of which come from the owners' own family farm.
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM - 2:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM - 2:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 2:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Sunday: Closed
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Daily specials that vary every day, with over 1,400 unique creations served to date.
Home of the famous "Croque Garcon Burger," named by Alton Brown as his favorite hamburger in the country.
Farm-to-table philosophy featuring ingredients from the owners' farm and local partners like Jon's Natural and Plum Creek Farm.
Award-winning menu including Poutine, Dragon Fries, and unique sandwiches like the Pulled Pork Roll.
Recognized as a James Beard Award semifinalist and frequently named "Best Burger in Omaha."
attractions.tips: Check their Facebook page for the daily special; arrive early for lunch as it gets very busy; they do not accept reservations; street parking is often available on 17th Street.
attractions.nearby: Old Market district, Gene Leahy Mall at The RiverFront, The Durham Museum, Joslyn Art Museum.

attractions.name: Holland Performing Arts Center
attractions.description: The Holland Performing Arts Center is a premier cultural facility in Omaha, renowned for its bold modern design and finely engineered acoustics that mirror the excellence of European 'shoebox' concert halls (Control Services Inc). It serves as a vibrant hub for a diverse range of live performances, including orchestral concerts, jazz, comedy, and family-friendly shows (Omaha Performing Arts).
attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday - Friday, 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM; Day of Show: Opens 90 minutes before showtime (Ticket Omaha). Door times vary by event, typically opening 1.5 to 2.5 hours before showtime (Ticket Omaha).
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Box Office), Daily for scheduled events (Omaha Performing Arts)
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event, typically starting as low as $10 with an average around $108 (Vivid Seats). Official tickets are sold through Ticket Omaha.
highlights: Home to the Omaha Symphony and host to national touring artists (Omaha Symphony).
Features the 2,000-seat Peter Kiewit Concert Hall and the flexible 350-seat Suzanne and Walter Scott Hall (Omaha Performing Arts).
Bold architectural design by Ennead Architects and HDR (Heritage Omaha).
Intimate performances at the Holland Music Club (formerly 1200 Club) (Omaha Performing Arts).
State-of-the-art acoustics designed by Kirkegaard Associates (Omaha Performing Arts).
On-site dining at the restaurant Zinc and various lounges (Omaha Performing Arts).
attractions.tips: Arrive early to navigate downtown construction and security lines (Ticket Omaha). All Omaha Performing Arts venues, including the Holland Center, are cashless as of August 2024 (Omaha Symphony). Metered street parking is free after 9 PM and all day on Sundays (Omaha Symphony).
attractions.nearby: Gene Leahy Mall, Old Market District, Durham Museum, Orpheum Theater, and Steelhouse Omaha (Visit Omaha, Hotels.com).

attractions.name: Omaha Children's Museum
attractions.description: The Omaha Children’s Museum is a nonprofit organization dedicated to engaging the imagination and creating excitement about learning through play. It features a variety of permanent and special interactive exhibits, including science, technology, engineering, art, and math (S.T.E.A.M.) experiences. Omaha Children's Museum
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Friday: 9 am – 4 pm; Saturday – Sunday: 9 am – 5 pm; Monday: Closed (except select holidays). Omaha Children's Museum
attractions.days_open: Tuesday – Sunday (open select Mondays during school holidays)
attractions.admission: Members: Free; Under 24 months: Free; Adults & Children (ages 2-59): $17 (plus tax); Seniors (60+): $16 (plus tax); Last hour of the day: $8 (plus tax). Omaha Children's Museum
highlights: Charlie Campbell Science & Technology Center
Imagination Playground with interactive role-play areas
Art Smart Center for creative expression
S.T.E.A.M. Cave for hands-on learning
Sandy’s Splish-Splash Garden (outdoor water play)
Bay Family Carousel and Train rides
attractions.tips: Visit on school weekdays in the afternoon for a quieter experience. Free parking is available in the lot on the south side of the building. Sensory bags containing headphones and fidget toys are available at the front desk. Omaha Children's Museum
attractions.nearby: The Rose Theater, Midtown Crossing, Durham Museum, Joslyn Art Museum. Midtown Crossing, Omaha Downtown

attractions.name: Omaha Symphony
attractions.description: Founded in 1921, the Omaha Symphony is a non-profit organization providing world-class orchestral performances, musical entertainment, and education for over a century. It serves over 225,000 people annually through more than 150 live performances across several series, including Masterworks, Symphony Joslyn, and LIVE with the Omaha Symphony. (Omaha Symphony, Omaha Symphony)
attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday - Friday, 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM; Concerts typically held in evenings (7:30 PM) or Sunday matinees (2:00 PM). (Omaha Symphony)
attractions.days_open: Monday through Friday (Box Office), Event days (Concerts) (Omaha Symphony)
attractions.admission: Varies by performance: Single tickets starting at $26; Subscription packages starting at $44 per person; Student and Teacher Rush tickets available for $10 (90 minutes before concert); Symphony Cathedral concerts $35 flat. (Omaha Symphony, Omaha Symphony)
highlights: Diverse concert series including Masterworks, Symphony Joslyn, and Family Series (Omaha Symphony)
Live movie score performances including Star Wars, Frozen, and Harry Potter (Omaha Symphony)
Performances at the architecturally stunning Holland Performing Arts Center (Omaha Symphony)
Educational initiatives like Mission Imagination and Concerts For Youth (Omaha Symphony)
Community collaborations such as 'Sounds of the City' at Gene Leahy Mall (Omaha Symphony)
Nationally recognized music directors and guest artists (Omaha Symphony)
attractions.tips: Purchase Student/Teacher Rush tickets 90 minutes before the show for just $10. Arrive early for downtown construction delays. The Holland Center is a cashless venue for food and drinks. Explore local partner restaurants like J. Gilbert's or Zinc for pre-concert dining. (Omaha Symphony, Omaha Symphony, Omaha Symphony)
attractions.nearby: Gene Leahy Mall, The Old Market, Orpheum Theater, Steelhouse Omaha, Joslyn Art Museum, Durham Museum (Omaha Symphony, Omaha Downtown)

attractions.name: Opera Omaha
attractions.description: Opera Omaha is the only professional opera company in Nebraska, known for producing high-caliber performances and innovative artist-led community programs. Founded in 1958, it has grown into one of the nation's most enterprising regional companies, staging major productions at the historic Orpheum Theater.
attractions.address: 1850 Farnam Street (Administrative), 409 S. 16th Street (Main Performance Venue)
attractions.hours: Administrative Office: Monday–Friday 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM (via Ticket Omaha box office). Performance times vary, typically 7:30 PM on Fridays and 2:00 PM on Sundays.
attractions.days_open: Administrative: Monday–Friday; Performances: Select Fridays and Sundays
attractions.admission: Prices vary by production. Individual tickets typically start around $30. Elevated Season Subscriptions are available for $75 per person. Many community events and conversations are Free.
highlights: Staged at the historic 2,600+ seat Orpheum Theater in downtown Omaha.
The only professional opera company serving the state of Nebraska.
Internationally recognized for commissioning modern American works and hosting world/U.S. premieres.
Resident orchestra is the Omaha Symphony Orchestra.
Home to the Holland Community Opera Fellowship, supporting community-minded artists.
Features the nation's only volunteer organization dedicated to operatic technical production, the Craftsman's Guild.
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30-45 minutes early to navigate downtown construction and enjoy the Orpheum's grandeur. While traditional formal wear is welcome, there is no strict dress code—modern audiences wear anything from jeans to evening gowns. Check the 'Companion’s Guide to Opera' on their website if you are new to the art form.
attractions.nearby: Orpheum Theater, Holland Performing Arts Center, Old Market Entertainment District, Gene Leahy Mall at The RiverFront, Joslyn Art Museum, Rose Theater.

attractions.name: Orpheum Theater
attractions.description: The Orpheum Theater is a historic cornerstone of Omaha’s cultural scene, originally opened in 1927 as a vaudeville house. Today, it is a premier 2,600-seat venue featuring ornate French Renaissance architecture, hosting Broadway tours, concerts, and Opera Omaha performances. Omaha Performing Arts, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Ticket Omaha Box Office: Mon-Fri 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM; Venue Box Office/Doors: Open 90 minutes before showtime. Omaha Performing Arts
attractions.admission: Varies by event; tickets available via Ticket Omaha. Ticket Omaha
highlights: Listed on the National Register of Historic Places. Wikipedia)
Features the ornate 2,600-seat Slosburg Hall with French Renaissance decor. Omaha Performing Arts
Home to the Omaha Performing Arts Broadway Season and Opera Omaha. Wikipedia)
Includes a historic Wurlitzer theatre organ. Wikipedia)
Connected to parking via the climate-controlled Skylink walkway. Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 60-90 minutes early to handle security and downtown traffic. While there is no formal dress code, large bags and backpacks are prohibited. Ticket Omaha
attractions.nearby: Old Market District, Holland Performing Arts Center, Steelhouse Omaha, Heartland of America Park, Bob Kerrey Pedestrian Bridge. OldMarket.com

attractions.name: Brickway Brewery & Distillery
attractions.description: Brickway Brewery & Distillery is Omaha's first combination brewery and distillery since Prohibition, located in the heart of the historic Old Market district. It offers a diverse selection of craft beers, such as their acclaimed Jalapeño Pineapple Pilsner, alongside award-winning spirits like their Sherry Cask Aged Single Malt Whisky. The establishment also features an on-site sushi restaurant, Foxy Sushi, providing a unique pairing of craft beverages and fresh cuisine. Brickway Brewery & Distillery, Visit Omaha
attractions.hours: Monday – Thursday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM; Friday & Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM; Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM Brickway Brewery & Distillery
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday (7 days a week) Brickway Brewery & Distillery
attractions.admission: Free entry to the tasting room; Tours are $25 per person (includes draft beer, guided tour, spirit tastings, and a take-home gift) Brickway Brewery & Distillery
highlights: Omaha's first brewery and distillery combination since Prohibition, opened in December 2013 Brickway Brewery & Distillery
Home to award-winning spirits, including multiple international gold medals for their Single Malt Whisky Brickway Brewery & Distillery
Features an on-site sushi restaurant, Foxy Sushi, offering fresh rolls and appetizers Brickway Brewery & Distillery
Houses the Borgata Lounge, a private event space with a classic speakeasy atmosphere Brickway Brewery & Distillery
Offers comprehensive Saturday afternoon tours that provide a behind-the-scenes look at production Brickway Brewery & Distillery
Produced and donated over 10,000 gallons of hand sanitizer to the community during the COVID-19 pandemic Greater Omaha Chamber
attractions.tips: Tours take place every Saturday at 1:30 PM and must be reserved online by Friday at 1:00 PM. Happy hour specials are available Monday through Friday from 3 PM to 6 PM and all day on Sunday. Don't miss trying their signature Jalapeño Pineapple Pilsner or the Sherry Cask Aged Single Malt, both of which are fan favorites. Brickway Brewery & Distillery
attractions.nearby: The Durham Museum, Bemis Center for Contemporary Arts, Gene Leahy Mall at The RiverFront, Heartland of America Park, and Old Market shops and galleries Tripadvisor

attractions.name: First National's Spirit of Nebraska's Wilderness and Pioneer Courage Park
attractions.description: This expansive sculpture installation spans six city blocks in downtown Omaha, featuring more than 100 bronze and stainless steel pieces that depict a 19th-century wagon train journey. The park integrates wildlife like stampeding bison and a flock of geese into the urban landscape, culminating in a stunning transition from bronze to modern stainless steel within the First National Bank Tower's atrium. (Visit Omaha, FNBO PDF)
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours a day, 7 days a week; Wintergarden Atrium typically follows business hours. (Visit Omaha, FNBO PDF)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Omaha, FNBO PDF)
highlights: One of the world's largest sculpture installations, with pieces at 1.25 times life size. (Visit Omaha)
Interactive display with four complete pioneer wagons and more than 100 individual pieces. (Omaha Exploration)
A 43,730-gallon fountain at Spirit of Nebraska's Wilderness Park featuring bronze Canada geese. (FNBO PDF)
Innovative use of the city's architecture, with sculptures attached to buildings, light posts, and traffic signals. (LandscapeArchitect.com)
The narrative concludes with stainless steel geese 'flying' through the 60-foot glass Wintergarden Atrium. (FNBO PDF)
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to walk alongside and interact with the sculptures; however, climbing on them is not permitted. Metered parking is available throughout the downtown area nearby. (Sarah's Notebook, Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: First National Bank Tower, Gene Leahy Mall at The Riverfront, Museum of Shadows, Old Market district. (Visit Omaha)

attractions.name: Gene Leahy Mall at The RiverFront
attractions.description: Gene Leahy Mall is an iconic 9.6-acre urban park in downtown Omaha that serves as the centerpiece of The RiverFront revitalization project. Redesigned in 2022 to be at street level, it features a performance pavilion, sculpture garden, and modern play areas while preserving historic artifacts like the beloved slides. It connects the historic Old Market with north downtown, providing a vibrant gathering space for the community (The RiverFront Omaha, OJB Landscape Architecture).
attractions.hours: Monday-Sunday: 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM (The RiverFront Omaha)
attractions.admission: Free (Omaha Symphony)
highlights: Performance Pavilion for live music and events (The RiverFront Omaha)
Sculpture Garden featuring diverse works of art (The RiverFront Omaha)
Iconic preserved playground slides near 11th Street (The RiverFront Omaha)
State-of-the-art destination playground (The RiverFront Omaha)
Modern dog park and game areas (The RiverFront Omaha)
Remote-control boat cove and interactive water features (Apple Maps)
attractions.tips: Metered parking and garages are available in the immediate proximity of the park. Visitors are encouraged to check the online events calendar for live performances at the pavilion and seasonal activities like the winter holiday light displays (The RiverFront Omaha).
attractions.nearby: Heartland of America Park, Lewis & Clark Landing, Old Market Historic District, Bob Kerrey Pedestrian Bridge, Kiewit Luminarium (Omaha Downtown, Finding the Universe)

attractions.name: Old Market Passageway Old Market

attractions.description: The Old Market Passageway is a unique, atmospheric multi-level complex located in a former alleyway, featuring brick-lined walkways, lush greenery, and enchanting twinkling lights. Transformed in the 1970s by the Mercer family, it houses iconic fine dining establishments, art galleries, and boutique shops, offering a European-style ambiance in the heart of Omaha. Old Market, Omaha Exploration

attractions.hours: Mon-Tue: 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Wed-Sat: 11:00 AM - 9:00 PM, Sun: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM (Note: Individual restaurant hours, such as V. Mertz and Trini's, may vary and extend later) Passageway Gallery, Tripadvisor
attractions.admission: Free Airial Travel

Fountain of the Erinyes on the ground level Omaha Exploration

Passageway Gallery, a local artist cooperative Passageway Gallery
Trini's Mexican Restaurant, an original 1977 tenant Omaha Exploration

The space is surprisingly wheelchair accessible. Don't miss the hidden Garden of the Zodiac located toward the back. Airial Travel, Locationscout.net

attractions.name: Omaha Farmers Market (Omaha Farmers Market)
attractions.description: The Omaha Farmers Market is a long-standing community tradition that connects local growers, artisans, and producers with the public. Operating in historic and vibrant neighborhoods, it provides access to fresh produce, unique handmade goods, and a variety of local food products while fostering a sense of community and connection. (Omaha Farmers Market)
attractions.address: 11th & Jackson Streets (Old Market); 67th & Mercy Road (Aksarben Village) (Omaha Farmers Market)
attractions.region: Old Market and Aksarben Village (Omaha Farmers Market)
attractions.hours: Saturdays (Old Market): 8:00am – 12:30pm; Sundays (Aksarben Village): 9:00am – 1:00pm; Holiday Market: 10:00am – 5:00pm (Omaha Farmers Market)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Omaha)
highlights: Over 120 local growers, chefs, and artists at the Aksarben Village location (Visit Omaha)
Historic setting on the charming brick streets of the Old Market district (Midtown Crossing)
Accepts SNAP and Double Up Food Bucks for fresh produce (Omaha Farmers Market)
Live music and street performers providing a lively atmosphere (Visit Omaha)
A wide selection of farm-fresh produce, artisanal cheeses, meats, and baked goods (Midtown Crossing)
Free bike valet services offered at the Sunday market in Aksarben Village (Omaha Farmers Market)
attractions.tips: Arrive early for the best selection of produce and to find parking; the first hour is free in some nearby garages. Bring small bills as some vendors may not accept cards, or visit the info booth for credit tokens. Pets are welcome but must be kept on a short, non-extendable leash at all times. (Omaha Farmers Market, Reddit)
attractions.nearby: Henry Doorly Zoo, Joslyn Art Museum, The Durham Museum, Stinson Park, and many local restaurants in the Old Market and Aksarben Village districts (Midtown Crossing)

attractions.description: The Omaha Farmers Market in the Old Market is a premier seasonal event featuring local growers, artisans, and food producers. Set against the backdrop of historic brick buildings and cobblestone streets, it offers a vibrant community atmosphere with fresh produce, live entertainment, and unique handcrafted goods.

attractions.name: Omaha Performing Arts (O-pa)
attractions.description: Omaha Performing Arts (O-pa) is a premier non-profit arts organization dedicated to providing world-class entertainment and educational programs in the Omaha region. It operates several iconic venues including the Holland Performing Arts Center and the historic Orpheum Theater, hosting a diverse array of Broadway shows, jazz, dance, and community events. Omaha Performing Arts
attractions.address: 1200 Douglas Street (Holland Center), 409 South 16th Street (Orpheum Theater)
attractions.hours: Administrative: Monday–Friday, 9:00 AM–5:00 PM; Ticket Omaha Box Office: Monday–Friday, 12:00 PM–5:00 PM; Performance Days: Box Office opens 90 minutes before showtime. Omaha Performing Arts
attractions.days_open: Monday–Friday (Box Office), plus event days (typically 7 days a week)
attractions.admission: Prices vary widely by event; Broadway shows typically range from $35 to $150+, while community events may be free. Season subscriptions and group discounts (10+ tickets) are available. Omaha Performing Arts, Ticket Omaha
highlights: Orpheum Theater: A historic 1927 vaudeville house restored for Broadway and major productions.
Holland Performing Arts Center: A modern facility known for its exceptional acoustics and design.
Steelhouse Omaha: A newer, flexible live music venue designed for standing-room performances.
Broadway Series: Hosting major touring productions in partnership with Broadway Across America.
Education Programs: Reaching over 1 million people through initiatives like the Nebraska Theater Academy.
Dick & Mary Holland Campus: A centralized arts hub in downtown Omaha connecting multiple world-class venues.
attractions.tips: Arrive early as doors typically open 60–90 minutes before showtime. Take advantage of the 'Skylink' walkway for the Orpheum Theater during cold weather. Check the 'Ticket Omaha' website for the official, lowest-priced tickets to avoid third-party markups. Omaha Performing Arts, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: The Durham Museum The Durham Museum
attractions.description: The Durham Museum is housed in Omaha's former Union Station, a National Historic Landmark and one of the best examples of Art Deco architecture in the country. It offers a fascinating look at the history of the region through permanent exhibits like restored train cars, the Byron Reed coin collection, and traveling exhibitions from the Smithsonian Institution. Visitors can also enjoy a vintage soda fountain and candy counter within the grand hall. The Durham Museum, NPS

attractions.region: Historic Old Market The Durham Museum

attractions.name: The Old Market District (Old Market Association)
attractions.description: The Old Market is Omaha's most historic neighborhood, featuring cobblestone streets and well-preserved brick buildings from the late 19th century. Once a bustling produce center, it has evolved into a vibrant district filled with unique shops, art galleries, and diverse dining options (Old Market Association).
attractions.address: Bound by Farnam, Jackson, 10th, and 13th Streets (National Park Service)
attractions.hours: District is open 24/7; business hours typically begin at 9:00 AM or 10:00 AM daily (Old Market Association, Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: Free (Old Market Association)
highlights: Cobblestone streets and historic brick architecture (Visit Omaha)
Old Market Passageway with its hidden shops and gardens (Visit Omaha)
First Friday self-guided art gallery tours (Old Market Association)
Horse-drawn carriage rides through the district (Wikipedia))
Eclectic shops like Homer’s Music and Hollywood Candy (Visit Omaha)

attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes to navigate the uneven cobblestone streets. Parking is available in nearby city garages or via the Park Omaha app for metered street spots. Be sure to check individual business hours as they vary by establishment (Old Market Association, Visit Omaha).
attractions.nearby: The Durham Museum, Gene Leahy Mall at The Riverfront, Kiewit Luminarium, and Omaha's Henry Doorly Zoo and Aquarium (Visit Omaha, Reddit)

attractions.name: Bob Kerrey Pedestrian Bridge
attractions.description: The Bob Kerrey Pedestrian Bridge is a 3,000-foot cable-stayed walkway that spans the Missouri River, elegantly connecting Omaha, Nebraska, and Council Bluffs, Iowa. As the first pedestrian bridge to link two states, it offers visitors stunning panoramic views of the Omaha skyline and the unique experience of standing in both Nebraska and Iowa simultaneously (Visit Omaha, TrailLink).
attractions.hours: Monday: Open 24 hours, Tuesday: Open 24 hours, Wednesday: Open 24 hours, Thursday: Open 24 hours, Friday: Open 24 hours, Saturday: Open 24 hours, Sunday: Open 24 hours (Evendo, Unleash Council Bluffs)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Omaha)
highlights: Standing in two states (Nebraska and Iowa) at once (Visit Omaha)
Unique S-curved cable-stayed architecture (Council Bluffs)
Vibrant nightly LED light displays (Wikipedia)
Access to over 150 miles of biking and walking trails (Visit Nebraska)
Home to the Omar the Troll sculpture (Visit Omaha)
Recently expanded with the 'Baby Bob' connection (Wikipedia)
attractions.tips: Visitors should try "bobbing"—taking a photo with one foot in each state. The bridge is best enjoyed at night for the light show or at sunset for the views. Be sure to check out the splash pad on the Omaha side during summer (Visit Omaha, Tripadvisor).

This picturesque neighborhood features distinctive cast-iron streetlights, lush gardens, and a central business district filled with unique shops and acclaimed restaurants. It is famously home to billionaire Warren Buffett and was recognized by the American Planning Association as one of the nation's 10 best neighborhoods.

It features two state-of-the-art performance spaces, the Hawks Mainstage and the Howard Drew Theatre, hosting 11 productions and special events annually (Visit Nebraska). The theatre is renowned for its rich history, having seen the acting debuts of icons like Henry Fonda and Marlon Brando (Wikipedia).

Stories of the diverse immigrant laborers who built the 'Magic City' Visit Omaha
Commemoration of Omaha's status as the world's largest livestock market History Nebraska
Part of a walkable and bikeable historic exploration route Visit Omaha
attractions.tips: The monuments are located outdoors along public sidewalks, so wear comfortable walking shoes and check the weather before visiting. Pair your historical tour with a visit to the nearby ethnic restaurants on South 24th Street for an authentic South Omaha experience Visit Omaha.
attractions.nearby: Livestock Exchange Building, South 24th Street Business District, El Museo Latino, Spring Lake Public Golf Course Visit Omaha

attractions.name: Omaha's Henry Doorly Zoo and Aquarium
attractions.description: Omaha's Henry Doorly Zoo and Aquarium is a world-renowned facility spanning over 160 acres and featuring more than 33,000 animals. It is consistently ranked as one of the best zoos in the nation and is famous for its record-setting indoor exhibits, including the world's largest indoor desert and one of the largest indoor rainforests. Source, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: April 1 - November 3: 9 a.m. - 5 p.m. daily (Buildings close at 6 p.m., some at 4 p.m.). November 4 - March 31: 10 a.m. - 4 p.m. daily (Buildings close at 5 p.m., some at 3 p.m. or 4 p.m.). Source
attractions.days_open: Open daily year-round (except Thanksgiving Day and Christmas Day). Source
attractions.admission: Summer (May 1 - Sept 30): Adults (12+) $33.95, Children (3-11) $25.95, Seniors (65+) $30.55, Children (2 and under) FREE, Military Adult $30.55, Military Child $23.35. Spring/Fall (Mar 1 - Apr 30, Oct 1 - Nov 30): Adults $28.95, Children $20.95, Seniors $25.95, Military Adult $25.95, Military Child $18.85. Winter (Dec 1 - Feb 28/29): Adults $21.95, Children $14.95, Seniors $19.75, Military Adult $19.75, Military Child $13.45. Source
highlights: Desert Dome: The world's largest indoor desert under a massive glazed geodesic dome.
Lied Jungle: One of the world's largest indoor rainforests featuring waterfalls and free-roaming animals.
Suzanne and Walter Scott Aquarium: A massive 1.2 million-gallon aquarium with a shark reef tunnel and penguin habitat.
Kingdoms of the Night: The world's largest nocturnal exhibit and indoor swamp located beneath the Desert Dome.
African Grasslands: A 28-acre habitat featuring elephants, giraffes, lions, and rhinos.
Owen Sea Lion Shores: A state-of-the-art sea lion and harbor seal habitat with an underwater viewing cavern.
attractions.tips: Arrive early and visit the Scott Aquarium first to avoid crowds. Bring a reusable water bottle to use the refill stations found in each building. Outside food and non-alcoholic beverages are permitted. Source, Reddit
attractions.nearby: The Durham Museum, Lauritzen Gardens, Omaha Children's Museum, Kenefick Park, and Little Bohemia neighborhood. Source, Wikipedia

attractions.name: South Omaha Main Street Historic District
attractions.description: The South Omaha Main Street Historic District is a nationally recognized architectural commercial area featuring over 30 buildings constructed between 1890 and 1910. Originally the urban core of the independent city of South Omaha, it reflects the area's rich history as a meatpacking hub and its diverse cultural heritage from Greek, Polish, Czech, and Latino communities. National Park Service, Wikipedia
attractions.address: Roughly S. 24th St. between M and O Sts. (District office at 4826 S 24th St.)
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: Open 24 hours (Individual business hours vary, e.g., International Bakery 6:30 AM - 9:00 PM; Rico Pan Bakery 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM) The Reader
attractions.admission: Free (The district is a public commercial area, though individual businesses or events like festivals may have costs.) Visit Omaha
attractions.phone: (402) 709-2586 (District Walking Tour) or (402) 444-5208 (City Planning Contact) Waze, National Park Service
highlights: Tree of Life: A cross-cultural symbolic sculpture located near 24th and L streets that welcomes visitors. Visit Omaha
Historic Murals: Vibrant public art along South 24th Street depicting the area's multi-ethnic history. American Planning Association
Packer's National Bank Building: A prominent NRHP-listed building that once served the local meatpacking industry. Wikipedia
Sidewalk Mosaics: A half-mile section of artistic sidewalk patterns stretching from L Street to Q Street. American Planning Association
El Museo Latino: The first Latino art and history museum in the Midwest, located within the district. Visit Omaha
Authentic Bakeries: Local landmarks like the Lithuanian Bakery (famous for 8-layer tortes) and International Bakery. Visit Omaha
attractions.tips: The district is best explored on foot to appreciate the sidewalk mosaics and historic architecture. Look for free parking available between L and Q Streets and at the Plaza de la Raza. Visit during the Cinco de Mayo festival in May or El Grito in September for the most vibrant atmosphere. South Omaha Business District, American Planning Association
attractions.nearby: El Museo Latino, Omaha Stockyards Monuments, Gerald R. Ford Birthsite and Gardens, Historic Vinton Street Commercial District Visit Omaha

attractions.name: Elkhorn (Omaha, Nebraska)
attractions.description: Elkhorn is a historic western neighborhood of Omaha, Nebraska, originally founded as an independent municipality in 1865. Known for its small-town atmosphere and highly-rated school district, it was annexed by Omaha in 2007 but maintains its unique identity through its historic "Olde Towne" district. (Wikipedia, Olde Towne Elkhorn)
attractions.address: 20601 Glenn St, Omaha, NE 68022 (Elkhorn Historical Society)
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Neighborhood area) (Wikipedia)
attractions.admission: Free (Apartments.com)
highlights: Historic Olde Towne district featuring brick-paved streets and local boutiques. Olde Towne Elkhorn
Ta-Ha-Zouka Park, offering extensive trails, sports fields, and outdoor recreation. Visit Omaha
Proximity to the Elkhorn River, providing scenic views and natural beauty. Wikipedia
Top-rated Elkhorn Public Schools, making it a premier residential area for families. Wikipedia
Annual Elkhorn Days community festival featuring parades and local festivities. Visit Nebraska
A blend of historic charm and modern growth within the Omaha metropolitan area. Olde Towne Elkhorn
attractions.tips: Explore the eclectic shops and local restaurants in the historic Olde Towne district for a unique small-town experience within the city. Visit during the summer to enjoy local community events and park amenities. (Olde Towne Elkhorn)
attractions.nearby: Village Pointe Shopping Center, Mount Michael Benedictine Abbey, Indian Creek Golf Course, Youngman Lake. (Visit Omaha, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Standing Bear Lake
attractions.description: Standing Bear Lake is a day-use recreation area in northwest Omaha named for the Ponca leader Chief Standing Bear. The park features a 135-acre lake surrounded by 3.3 miles of paved trails, including a unique floating bridge that completes the loop. Visitors can enjoy a variety of outdoor activities such as fishing, no-wake boating, paddleboarding, and picnicking in a serene natural setting (Visit Omaha, Rollocks).
attractions.hours: Daily, 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM (Visit Omaha, Rollocks)
attractions.admission: Free (Visit Omaha, Airial Travel)
highlights: 3.3-mile paved multi-use trail loop encircling the lake (Rollocks)
Unique floating bridge trail that allows for a complete 3-mile circuit (Airial Travel, KMTV)
No-wake boating lake ideal for kayaking, canoeing, and paddleboarding (Rollocks)
Abundant fishing opportunities for largemouth bass, bluegill, and crappie (Rollocks)
Field for radio-controlled model airplanes (Visit Omaha)
Modern playground and multiple picnic shelters for families (Rollocks)
attractions.tips: Arrive early on weekends to secure the best fishing spots and parking, as it can get crowded. Note that swimming is strictly prohibited due to water quality and safety concerns. Anglers must have a valid Nebraska fishing permit before casting a line (Rollocks, Reddit).
attractions.nearby: Stone Creek Public Golf Course, Eagle Run Public Golf Course, Saddlebrook Park, and several restaurants like Salted Edge (Visit Omaha, Facebook, TrailLink)

attractions.name: Tranquility Park
attractions.description: Tranquility Park is one of Omaha's largest and most ecologically significant public spaces, serving as a major hub for both organized sports and unstructured outdoor recreation. The 340-acre park features an extensive 9-mile natural surface trail system for mountain biking and hiking, alongside a massive complex of soccer and baseball fields. It is currently undergoing a $54 million redevelopment to enhance its facilities and transition into a premier regional youth multi-sport complex (Omaha Parks, THOR Trails).
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM (Waze)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)
highlights: Extensive 9-mile natural surface trail network for mountain biking, hiking, and running
Large soccer complex featuring multiple synthetic turf and grass fields
Baseball and softball diamonds used for local and regional tournaments
Adjacent to the Moylan Iceplex for year-round ice skating and hockey
Playgrounds and tennis courts for community family use
Ecologically significant natural areas including a historic tree nursery
attractions.tips: Visitors to the mountain bike trails should bring bug spray and wear a helmet for safety. During peak soccer season, parking can be extremely limited; the Moylan Iceplex parking lot is often used as a secondary access point for the trails. Be aware that the park is undergoing significant renovations through 2027, so check the official city website for any temporary area closures (Tripadvisor, 3 News Now).
attractions.nearby: Standing Bear Lake, Heron Haven Nature & Wildlife Area, Boys Town, Westroads Mall, Zorinsky Lake and Recreation Area (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Lee G. Simmons Wildlife Safari Park
attractions.description: Lee G. Simmons Wildlife Safari Park is a four-mile, drive-through North American wildlife adventure where visitors can encounter native animals like elk, bison, and waterfowl in a natural prairie and wetland setting. Managed by the Omaha Henry Doorly Zoo and Aquarium, the park also features hiking trails, an eagle aviary, and a visitor center with educational exhibits. Wildlife Safari Park, Visit Omaha
attractions.hours: 9 a.m. - 5 p.m. daily (April - October). Wildlife Safari Park
attractions.days_open: Saisonally from April to October Wildlife Safari Park, Tripadvisor
attractions.admission: Adults (12+): $14, Children (3-11): $12, Seniors (65+): $13, Children 2 and under: FREE, Military Adult: $13, Military Child: $11. Wildlife Safari Park
highlights: Bison Plains featuring a herd of American bison and pronghorn. Wildlife Safari Park
Wolf Canyon with a boardwalk overlooking a pack of gray wolves. Wildlife Safari Park
Eagle Aviary, one of the largest in the nation, housing rescued bald eagles. Wildlife Safari Park
Prairie Dog Town where visitors can observe the active social lives of black-tailed prairie dogs. Wildlife Safari Park
Hiking Trails including the Wolf Woods and Frog Lake trails for on-foot exploration. Wildlife Safari Park
attractions.tips: Purchase timed tickets in advance as non-member tickets are arrival-window dependent; the drive usually takes 1-2 hours but can be extended by using the hiking trails. Wildlife Safari Park, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Lee G. Simmons Wildlife Safari Park (Official Website)
attractions.description: The Lee G. Simmons Wildlife Safari Park is a 440-acre, four-mile drive-through adventure featuring North American wildlife in their natural habitats. Visitors can observe animals like bison, elk, and wolves while exploring scenic prairies and wetlands located just outside of Omaha. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: 9 a.m. - 5 p.m. Daily (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Adults (12+): $14, Children (3-11): $12, Seniors (65+): $13, Children 2 and under: FREE, Military Adult: $13, Military Child: $11. (Official Website)
highlights: Bison Plains: A 40-acre habitat for a massive herd of American bison. (Omaha Guide)
Wolf Canyon: An overlook and hiking trail to view gray wolves and black bears. (Omaha Guide)
Eagle Aviary: Home to bald eagles and other native birds. (Official Website)
Elk Meadows: The first and last exhibit featuring a large elk herd. (Omaha Guide)
Prairie Dog Town: An underground colony near the visitor center. (Official Website)
attractions.tips: Purchase timed arrival tickets online in advance to guarantee entry, as same-day availability is limited. Bring binoculars for better animal viewing from your car and wear comfortable shoes if you plan to explore the hiking trails like Pawnee Creek. (Official Website, Omaha Guide)
attractions.nearby: Strategic Air Command & Aerospace Museum, Eugene T. Mahoney State Park (Omaha Guide)

attractions.name: Aksarben Village
attractions.description: Aksarben Village is Omaha’s premier mixed-use entertainment and shopping complex, featuring a vibrant blend of retail shops, diverse restaurants, and residential units. Built on the site of the former Ak-Sar-Ben arena, it serves as a central hub for community life with green spaces, a movie theater, and regular public events.

attractions.admission: Free (The development and Stinson Park are free; individual businesses like the cinema or food hall vendors have their own pricing) Aksarben Village, Maha Music Festival

Aksarben Cinema: A 10-screen theater offering films, local beer, and special event screenings.
Retail & Dining: Home to 19+ restaurants and bars ranging from sushi to American bistros.
Keystone Trail Access: Direct connection to local biking and walking trails for outdoor enthusiasts.
Proximity to UNO: Located adjacent to the University of Nebraska Omaha’s south campus and Baxter Arena.

attractions.name: Beercade (Omaha)
attractions.description: Beercade is Omaha's original arcade bar, located in the heart of the Benson entertainment district. It combines the nostalgia of a retro arcade with the atmosphere of a neighborhood pub, offering over 50 draft beers and a vast collection of vintage video games and pinball machines across two floors (Beercade Official).

attractions.admission: Free entry; games typically cost 25¢–$1; $3 cover for basement access on Friday and Saturday nights (TripAdvisor, Yelp)

A rotating list of 30+ craft beers and a fully stocked bar for cocktails and shots (Beercade Official)
attractions.tips: The venue is family-friendly daily before 7:00 PM, transitioning to a strictly 21+ adult environment in the evening. While no food is served on-site, visitors are encouraged to bring in local takeout from nearby Benson restaurants (Beercade Official).

attractions.name: Bellevue University
attractions.description: Bellevue University is a private, non-profit institution founded in 1966 that specializes in providing flexible and affordable education for adult learners. It serves over 10,000 students through a variety of undergraduate and graduate programs offered both online and at its suburban campus in Bellevue, Nebraska. The university is nationally recognized for its commitment to military veterans and its focus on career-relevant academic paths. Wikipedia, Bellevue University

attractions.name: Benson (Neighborhood)
attractions.description: Benson is a historic neighborhood and official Creative District in Omaha, Nebraska, known for its vibrant nightlife, indie music scene, and eclectic dining. Originally platted in 1887 and annexed by Omaha in 1917, the area centers on the historic Maple Street corridor, which is lined with unique shops, art galleries, and popular concert venues like The Waiting Room Lounge. Today, it serves as a quirky-cool cultural hub that maintains a family-friendly atmosphere during the day while transforming into a buzzing entertainment district at night.
attractions.address: Maple Street (centered between North 59th and North 63rd Streets), Omaha, NE 68104
attractions.hours: Individual business hours vary; the neighborhood is accessible 24/7. Common business hours: Tue-Sat 11:00 AM - Close, Sun 12:00 PM - Close (e.g., Benson Brewery). Nightlife venues typically stay open until 2:00 AM.
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Individual business hours vary)
attractions.admission: Free (Individual businesses and venues like Benson Theatre and The Waiting Room have separate ticket prices, often ranging from Free to $30+)
highlights: The Waiting Room Lounge: A renowned indie music venue hosting national and local acts.
Benson Creative District: Omaha’s first designated Creative District, home to numerous murals and galleries.
Benson First Friday (BFF): A monthly neighborhood-wide celebration of arts, music, and community.
Benson Commercial Historic District: A National Register-listed area featuring 41 contributing historic buildings.
Beercade: A nostalgic bar and arcade pairing retro games with over 50 brews on tap.
Yoshitomo: A highly-acclaimed sushi restaurant led by James Beard finalist Chef David Utterback.
attractions.tips: Visit on the first Friday of the month for 'BFF' (Benson First Friday) to experience the full creative energy of the neighborhood. Parking can be scarce on weekend nights; try the public lot south of 61st and Maple or look for street parking on residential side streets south of Maple. For a quieter experience, visit during weekday afternoons to enjoy the shops and bakeries without the crowds.
attractions.nearby: Benson Park (7028 Military Ave), Omaha Public Library - Benson Branch (6015 Binney St), Gallagher Park (Pool & Splash Pad), Benson Community Center, and the nearby Dundee neighborhood.

attractions.name: Boys Town National Research Hospital and Village of Boys Town Campus (Boys Town Hospital, Boys Town)
attractions.description: The Village of Boys Town is a National Historic Landmark District and home to the world-renowned Boys Town National Research Hospital, specializing in pediatric hearing and communication disorders. The campus serves as the national headquarters for an organization dedicated to saving children and healing families through a variety of residential, medical, and research programs. Visitors can explore a historic village featuring museums, chapels, and gardens that detail the legacy of founder Father Edward J. Flanagan. (Boys Town Hospital, Boys Town)
attractions.address: 14100 Crawford Street, Boys Town, NE 68010 (Boys Town)
attractions.hours: Hall of History: Mon-Sat 10:00 AM - 4:30 PM, Sun 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM; Visitors Center & Gift Shop: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM (Closed Sat-Sun); Overlook Café: Mon-Thu 6:30 AM - 1:30 PM (Closed Fri-Sun); Dowd Chapel Mass: Weekdays 11:40 AM, Sun 8:00 AM, 9:30 AM, 11:00 AM (Boys Town)

World’s Largest Stamp Ball located at the Leon Myers Stamp Center (O'Daniel Honda)

attractions.tips: Start your visit at the Visitors Center to pick up a campus map and information on self-guided tours. Download the "Boys Town Visitor Tours" app for an interactive experience as you drive through the village. (Boys Town)
attractions.nearby: Omaha’s Henry Doorly Zoo and Aquarium, The Old Market, Strategic Air Command and Aerospace Museum, Omaha Children's Museum (Visit Omaha)

attractions.name: Charles Schwab Field Omaha
attractions.description: Charles Schwab Field Omaha is a premier ballpark in Omaha, Nebraska, serving as the permanent home of the NCAA Men’s College World Series since it opened in 2011. The stadium also hosts the Creighton Bluejays baseball team and various other high-profile events like the Big Ten Baseball Tournament and major concerts. With a seating capacity of 24,000 and modern fan amenities, it remains the center of collegiate baseball culture in the United States. (Charles Schwab Field Omaha, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Dowd Memorial Chapel of the Immaculate Conception (Boys Town)
attractions.description: Dowd Memorial Chapel of the Immaculate Conception is a stunning 15th-century Gothic-style church that stands at the heart of the Village of Boys Town. Completed in 1941, it serves as a spiritual sanctuary for the community and houses the final resting place of Boys Town founder, Father Edward J. Flanagan. (Boys Town)

attractions.name: Eugene T. Mahoney State Park
attractions.description: Eugene T. Mahoney State Park is a premier year-round destination located on the Platte River, offering a vast array of recreational activities and modern facilities. The park features a state-of-the-art aquatic center, an observation tower with panoramic views, and diverse lodging options including a 40-room lodge and over 50 cabins. It serves as a popular hub for family reunions and outdoor adventure, conveniently situated between Omaha and Lincoln. Wikipedia, Visit Omaha
attractions.hours: Park Grounds: Mon-Sun: 12:00 AM - 11:59 PM (Open 24/7). Specific attraction hours (Aquatic Center, Activity Center, Marina) vary by season. Tripadvisor

Indoor Activity Center with a 42-foot climbing wall and playground
Denman and Mary Mallory Kountze Memorial Theater for live performances
attractions.tips: Book cabins and lodge rooms up to one year in advance for peak summer weekends; visit the observation tower at sunset for spectacular photography opportunities; bring binoculars for birdwatching, particularly during migration seasons. Oh My! Omaha

attractions.name: Florence Mill (Winter Quarters Mill Museum)
attractions.description: The Florence Mill is a historic gristmill originally built in 1846 by Mormon pioneers during their westward migration. Today, it serves as a multi-purpose cultural site housing the Winter Quarters Mill Museum and the Art-Loft Gallery, while also hosting a popular seasonal farmers market. It is recognized on the National Register of Historic Places as Nebraska's oldest business site. (Official Website, VisitNebraska)
attractions.hours: Thursday: 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Sunday: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Seasonal: Mid-May to early October) (VisitNebraska)

Listed on the National Register of Historic Places as the Weber Mill. (National Park Service)

attractions.tips: Visit on a Sunday between June and September to enjoy the Farmers Market alongside the museum and gallery; be mindful that the site is a seasonal attraction and closed during the winter months. (VisitOmaha)
attractions.nearby: Mormon Trail Center at Winter Quarters, Mormon Pioneer Cemetery, Winter Quarters Nebraska Temple, Hummel Park, General Crook House Museum (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Fontenelle Forest Nature Center
attractions.description: Fontenelle Forest is one of Nebraska's oldest conservation organizations, featuring over 2,100 acres of forest, prairie, and wetlands along the Missouri River. It offers 24 miles of trails, a mile-long ADA-accessible boardwalk, and unique wildlife experiences such as the Raptor Woodland Refuge. Fontenelle Forest Official Website, National Park Service

attractions.name: Freedom Park (City of Omaha Parks)
attractions.description: Freedom Park is an outdoor naval museum and park situated on the banks of the Missouri River, displaying a diverse collection of military aircraft and artillery (Wikipedia)). Its hallmark exhibits are the USS Hazard (AM-240), a World War II minesweeper, and the USS Marlin (SST-2), a Cold War-era training submarine (City of Omaha Parks). This 12-acre site allows visitors to board historic vessels and gain a tangible connection to naval and aviation history (Omaha Exploration).
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: Closed, Wednesday: Closed, Thursday: Closed, Friday: Closed, Saturday: 10:00 am – 3:00 pm (May–October), Sunday: Closed (City of Omaha Parks)
attractions.admission: Free (City of Omaha Parks)

USS Marlin (SST-2), a unique Cold War-era target and training submarine (City of Omaha Parks)
Military aircraft including a Douglas A-4C Skyhawk and HH-52A Seaguard helicopter (Douglas County Historical Society)
An extensive outdoor display of naval guns, missiles, and rocket launchers (Omaha.net)
The "Anchor and Propeller Garden" featuring various maritime artifacts (Douglas County Historical Society)

It is recommended to wear comfortable walking shoes for the 12-acre outdoor site and to check the weather beforehand as the park may close for inclement conditions (Roadside America).

attractions.name: General Crook House Museum (Douglas County Historical Society)
attractions.description: The General Crook House Museum is an authentically restored Italianate style home built in 1879 for Civil War and National Indian Wars hero General George Crook. Located on the historic Fort Omaha grounds, the museum features Victorian-era antiques and exhibits focused on the history of Douglas County and the landmark trial of Standing Bear v. Crook. (Visit Nebraska)

attractions.hours: Monday – Friday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Saturday – Sunday: 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM (Douglas County Historical Society)

highlights: Authentically restored 1879 Italianate-style military residence (Douglas County Historical Society)
Extensive collection of 1880s Victorian-era period furnishings and decorative arts (Visit Nebraska)

attractions.tips: If the museum door is locked during regular operating hours, visitors are encouraged to ring the doorbell for assistance from staff. (Airial Travel, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Great Plains Black History Museum (official website)
attractions.description: The Great Plains Black History Museum is a nationally renowned institution dedicated to preserving, celebrating, and educating the public on the contributions and achievements of African Americans throughout the Great Plains. It houses an extensive collection of over 100,000 items, including periodicals, manuscripts, and photographs documenting the Black experience west of the Mississippi River (official website, Wikipedia).

Its original taproom is housed in a beautifully restored 1917 building in the Benson neighborhood that served as Olson’s Meat Market for over 60 years. The brewery is famous for its flagship Vanilla Bean Blonde Ale and maintains a rotating selection of over a dozen hand-crafted beers on tap. Infusion Brewing Co., City Brew Tours

attractions.name: Lewis and Clark Monument Park (Lewis and Clark Monument and Scenic Overlook)
attractions.description: Dedicated in 1936, this 35-acre park honors the 1804 expedition of Lewis and Clark and their historic meeting with Otoe and Missouri tribesmen. Perched on a high bluff in the unique Loess Hills, the site offers breathtaking panoramic views of the Missouri River, the Council Bluffs area, and the Omaha skyline. (Official Website, NPS)
attractions.hours: Monday–Sunday: 8:00 AM – 10:00 PM (Unleash Council Bluffs)
attractions.admission: Free (Yelp, Airial Travel)

Panoramic westward views of the Omaha skyline and Missouri River valley
Access to extensive hiking and mountain biking trails maintained by THOR
Observation deck providing a unique vantage point for plane spotting at Eppley Airfield
Seasonal picnic facilities and public restrooms
Historic site commemorating the parley between explorers and Native American tribes
attractions.tips: Visit at sunset for the most spectacular views and vibrant sky colors. Bring binoculars to watch flights at the nearby airport, and be prepared for windy conditions due to the park's elevated position. Wear sturdy shoes if you plan to explore the steep hiking or biking trails. (Airial Travel, Tripadvisor)

attractions.description: The Mormon Trail Center at Winter Quarters is a visitors’ center featuring exhibits that interpret the story of the westward migration of Latter-day Saint pioneers between 1846 and 1848. (Official Website, Wikipedia)

Interactive electronic and fiber optic exhibits detailing the migration (Visit Nebraska)

Artifacts including handcarts used by early settlers (Nebraska Traveler)
Adjacency to the historic Mormon Pioneer Cemetery and the Winter Quarters Temple (Wikipedia)

Reorganized in 2024 under new leadership, the festival continues a decades-long tradition of making the works of William Shakespeare accessible to the community in a scenic park setting.
attractions.address: Elmwood Park, 802 S 60th St (Offices: 2500 California Plaza, Omaha, NE 68178)
attractions.hours: Performances typically start at 8:00 PM; preshow activities and seminars begin at 6:30 PM or 7:00 PM.

Intricate interior mosaics and murals in the Rotunda and Great Hall
14th-floor observation decks offering panoramic views of Lincoln
Nation's only Unicameral (one-house) Legislature chamber

attractions.name: Platte River State Park
attractions.description: Nestled halfway between Omaha and Lincoln, this scenic state park features charming vintage cabins, a picturesque waterfall, and extensive hiking and biking trails. Visitors can enjoy spectacular vistas from two observation towers and participate in diverse activities ranging from paddleboating to shooting sports (Nebraska Game & Parks Commission).
attractions.hours: Grounds: Open daily year-round for day use. Park Office: Generally 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM daily (Tripadvisor, VisitNebraska.com)
attractions.admission: Nebraska Resident: Daily $7, Annual $35; Non-Resident: Daily $14, Annual $70 (Nebraska Game & Parks Commission)
highlights: Two observation towers (Lincoln Journal Tower and Walter Scott Jr. Observation Tower) offering bird's-eye views of the Platte River Basin (Nebraska Game & Parks Commission)
Picturesque Stone Creek Falls waterfall accessible via a short hiking trail (TravelAwaits)
Roger G. Sykes Outdoor Heritage Education Complex for archery, firearm shooting, and tomahawk throwing (Nebraska Game & Parks Commission)
Over 10 miles of trails, including some of the best mountain biking in the region with features like log rides and wall rides (Nebraska Game & Parks Commission)
Jenny Newman Lake for catch-and-release fishing and paddleboat rentals (Oh My! Omaha)
Diverse lodging options including glamping cabins, vintage camper cabins, and tepees (Nebraska Game & Parks Commission)
attractions.tips: Climb the metal observation tower for the most panoramic views of the river. If visiting with kids, the spray park is a popular summer feature, though it closes seasonally after Labor Day. Purchasing your vehicle entry permit online in advance can streamline your arrival at the park (TravelAwaits, Oh My! Omaha).
attractions.nearby: Strategic Air Command & Aerospace Museum, Wildlife Safari Park, Schramm Education Center, and Eugene T. Mahoney State Park (TravelAwaits)

It features the flagship offices of the influential Saddle Creek Records label and its affiliated music venue, The Slowdown, which serves as a permanent hub for live rock and indie music. The development was instrumental in revitalizing the NoDo area and creating a dedicated space for the 'Omaha Sound.' The Slowdown History, Saddle Creek Records About

attractions.name: Slowdown
attractions.description: Slowdown is a premier live music venue and bar located in Omaha's North Downtown district, developed by Saddle Creek Records to provide a permanent home for the city's vibrant music scene. Named after the influential local band Slowdown Virginia, the mixed-use complex features two distinct performance spaces and hosts everything from rock concerts to community events. (Slowdown History, Wikipedia))
attractions.hours: Typically opens 1 hour prior to each event and stays open until approximately 2:00 AM. The venue is closed on nights without scheduled events or private bookings. (Slowdown FAQ)
attractions.days_open: Varies based on event schedule; typically 3-4 nights per week. (Slowdown FAQ)
attractions.admission: Varies by event; typically $10-$50+ per ticket. Free for specific events like the "Playing With Fire" series. Cash purchases at the box office are fee-free. (Slowdown Events, Slowdown FAQ)
highlights: Features two distinct performance spaces: an intimate Front Room and a larger Main Room for major acts. (Wikipedia))
Developed and owned by Saddle Creek Records, a cornerstone of the Omaha music scene. (Wikipedia))
Hosts a diverse range of entertainment including live rock concerts, dance parties, and community "Science Café" events. (Slowdown FAQ, Yelp)
Includes a full bar with an extensive craft beer list and a dedicated merchandise shop. (Slowdown Menu)
Offers 138 free parking spots in a dedicated lot behind the building, a rare convenience in downtown Omaha. (Slowdown Locate)
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets in advance online or with cash at the box office to avoid service fees. Ensure you have a valid photo ID, which is mandatory for entry regardless of age. Arrive early if you prefer seating, as most shows are general admission with limited first-come, first-served tables. (Slowdown FAQ)

attractions.name: Strategic Air Command & Aerospace Museum
attractions.description: The Strategic Air Command & Aerospace Museum is a 300,000-square-foot facility dedicated to preserving the history of the Strategic Air Command and its role in the Cold War. It houses an extensive collection of historic aircraft, missiles, and space artifacts, including iconic bombers and reconnaissance planes, while offering immersive STEM education and interactive exhibits.
attractions.hours: Winter (Sept. 2 - May 24): Mon-Fri 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Sat 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Sun 11:00 AM - 5:00 PM. Summer (May 25 - Sept. 7): 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM daily. Official Website
attractions.admission: General Admission: Adults (13-64) $17.50, Senior Citizens (65+) $13.50, Military $13.50, Children (Ages 4-12) $9.00, Children (3 & under) Free. Last hour discount: 50% off. Planetarium: Adults $5, Seniors/Military/Children $3. Official Website
highlights: Lockheed SR-71 Blackbird displayed in a glass atrium

attractions.name: Sunken Gardens
attractions.description: Sunken Gardens is an iconic 1.5-acre floral display garden that has been a treasured part of the Lincoln landscape since it was first developed in 1930 during the Great Depression. It is the only Nebraska garden listed in the "300 Best Gardens to Visit in the United States and Canada," featuring more than 30,000 individual plants arranged in intricate, themed patterns that change every year. Visitors can explore various sections including the Perennial Garden, the Healing Garden, and lily ponds while enjoying artistic sculptures and cascading water features (City of Lincoln, Lincoln Parks Foundation).
attractions.address: 2601 D Street, Lincoln, NE 68502 (City of Lincoln)
attractions.region: Near South / Antelope Park area (Homes.com, TripAdvisor)
attractions.hours: Daily: 5:00 AM – 11:00 PM (City of Lincoln)
attractions.admission: Free; donations are accepted (City of Lincoln, Lincoln Parks Foundation)
highlights: Annual Floral Theme: Each year features a new design, such as the 2026 quilt-inspired theme (City of Lincoln).
Healing Garden: A tranquil white-themed garden inspired by Sissinghurst Castle in England (Midwest Association of Independent Inns).
Koi Ponds & Lily Pads: Multiple water features including restored lily ponds and a cascading waterfall (City of Lincoln).
"Inspiration" Sculpture: A prominent public art piece by artist Mary Buckman located within the gardens (City of Lincoln).
Perennial Garden: A lush upper-level section showcasing sun and shade plants for home garden inspiration (Midwest Association of Independent Inns).
attractions.tips: Visit during the "golden hour" (early morning or late afternoon) for the most dramatic lighting on the flowers. Note that public restrooms are only open seasonally from April to October. Parking is available in a small lot off 26th and D Street, with overflow parking on adjacent neighborhood streets (City of Lincoln, Midwest Association of Independent Inns).

attractions.name: University of Nebraska at Omaha (UNO)
attractions.description: The University of Nebraska at Omaha (UNO) is a public research university located in the heart of Omaha, Nebraska, and is part of the University of Nebraska system. Founded in 1908, it is classified as an R2: Doctoral University with high research activity and offers more than 200 programs of study across six colleges. The university is known for its strong metropolitan mission, integrating student life with the city's professional and cultural landscape (University of Nebraska Omaha, Wikipedia).

attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (standard business hours); limited Saturday events

Visitor parking is available in Lot E on the Dodge Campus, which is staffed with an attendant during business hours. It is recommended to explore both the Dodge Campus and the Scott Campus to see the full range of academic facilities (University of Nebraska Omaha).
